flags.concern: Step 3 changes the context's ideal editor height, but the rendered editor pane is further reduced in `EditorPaneTab`, so this plan will not reliably produce a 70% editor when the generations pane is also visible.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx:141-169` computes `effectiveEditorPaneHeight`, but `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:41-47` then subtracts `effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight` again before using that value for the tab position and pane `height` at `:98` and `:132`. On common viewports, lowering the ideal from 90% to 70% in `PanesContext` either has no effect after `getEffectivePaneHeights(...)` clamps it, or still gets reduced again by the generations offset, so the user-facing pane height will not match the stated 70% target.

flags.concern: Step 2's proposed effect is keyed only to `location.pathname`, so it can miss the exact case where the persisted editor lock hydrates after mount while the user is already on `/tools/video-editor`.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/contexts/usePaneLockPolicyState.ts:26-56` initializes from `useUserUIState(...)` and then reapplies `paneLocks` when loading completes. If `EditorPaneTab` mounts on the editor route before that hydration finishes, an effect with deps `[location.pathname]` can run once while `pane.isLocked` is still false and never rerun when `locks.editor` later becomes true. The existing generations-pane route-unlock logic avoids this by depending on both route state and live pane state: `src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/hooks/useGenerationsPaneController.ts:234-238`.

flags.concern: Step 1 says to just verify `LayoutMainContent.test.tsx` still passes, but the repository already has a test asserting the current shifting behavior, so the plan is missing an explicit test update.
flags.evidence: `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx:107-113` currently expects `paddingTop: '360px'` when the editor pane is locked. Removing the padding in `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx:40` will fail that test until the assertion is rewritten.
